questions.q: What is the main purpose of a function in programming?
opts: (A) To store data
(B) To perform a specific task
(C) To declare variables
(D) To control the flow of a program
questions.explanation: A function is a block of code that performs a specific task. It helps to reduce code duplication and make programs more modular.

questions.q: What is the difference between a function and a method?
opts: (A) A function is a block of code, while a method is a function within a class
(B) A function is a reusable block of code, while a method is a non-reusable block of code
(C) A function is a code that takes arguments, while a method is a code that does not take arguments
(D) A function is a code that modifies variables, while a method is a code that does not modify variables
questions.explanation: A function is a standalone block of code, while a method is a function within a class that is used to perform operations on objects of that class.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the return statement in a function?
opts: (A) To end the function execution
(B) To pass data to another function
(C) To return data from the function
(D) To declare a variable
questions.explanation: The return statement is used to return data from a function, which can then be used by the caller.

questions.q: What is the difference between a parameter and an argument in a function?
opts: (A) A parameter is the data passed to a function, while an argument is the data received by the function
(B) A parameter is the data received by a function, while an argument is the data passed to the function
(C) A parameter is a variable, while an argument is a constant
(D) A parameter is a function, while an argument is a variable
questions.explanation: A parameter is a variable in the function definition, while an argument is the actual value passed to the function when it is called.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the keyword 'this' in a function?
opts: (A) To refer to the current object
(B) To refer to the parent object
(C) To refer to the global object
(D) To refer to the null object
questions.explanation: The keyword 'this' is used to refer to the current object within a function, allowing the function to access its properties and methods.

questions.q: What is the difference between a function prototype and a function declaration?
opts: (A) A function prototype is a declaration of a function, while a function declaration is a definition of a function
(B) A function prototype is a definition of a function, while a function declaration is a declaration of a function
(C) A function prototype is a code that takes arguments, while a function declaration is a code that does not take arguments
(D) A function prototype is a code that modifies variables, while a function declaration is a code that does not modify variables
questions.explanation: A function prototype is a declaration of a function, which includes the function name and parameter list, while a function declaration is a definition of a function, which includes the function body.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the keyword 'default' in a function?
opts: (A) To specify a default value for a parameter
(B) To specify a default value for a variable
(C) To specify a default value for a constant
(D) To specify a default value for a function
questions.explanation: The keyword 'default' is used to specify a default value for a parameter in a function, which is used when no value is passed for that parameter.

questions.q: What is the difference between a function and a lambda function?
opts: (A) A function is a block of code, while a lambda function is a small block of code
(B) A function is a reusable block of code, while a lambda function is a non-reusable block of code
(C) A function is a code that takes arguments, while a lambda function is a code that does not take arguments
(D) A function is a code that modifies variables, while a lambda function is a code that does not modify variables
questions.explanation: A function is a block of code that can be reused, while a lambda function is a small block of code that is used to perform a simple operation.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the keyword 'const' in a function?
opts: (A) To specify that a function does not modify variables
(B) To specify that a function modifies variables
(C) To specify that a function returns a value
(D) To specify that a function takes arguments
questions.explanation: The keyword 'const' is used to specify that a function does not modify variables, which can help prevent unintended modifications.

questions.q: What is the difference between a function and a generator?
opts: (A) A function is a block of code, while a generator is a block of code that returns values one at a time
(B) A function is a reusable block of code, while a generator is a non-reusable block of code
(C) A function is a code that takes arguments, while a generator is a code that does not take arguments
(D) A function is a code that modifies variables, while a generator is a code that does not modify variables
questions.explanation: A function is a block of code that returns a single value, while a generator is a block of code that returns values one at a time, using the 'yield' keyword.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the keyword 'yield' in a generator?
opts: (A) To return a value from a generator
(B) To pass data to another generator
(C) To specify a default value for a parameter
(D) To specify a value to be returned from a function
questions.explanation: The keyword 'yield' is used to return a value from a generator, which is used to produce a sequence of values.

questions.q: What is the difference between a function and a coroutine?
opts: (A) A function is a block of code, while a coroutine is a block of code that can be paused and resumed
(B) A function is a reusable block of code, while a coroutine is a non-reusable block of code
(C) A function is a code that takes arguments, while a coroutine is a code that does not take arguments
(D) A function is a code that modifies variables, while a coroutine is a code that does not modify variables
questions.explanation: A function is a block of code that executes sequentially, while a coroutine is a block of code that can be paused and resumed, using the 'await' keyword.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the keyword 'await' in a coroutine?
opts: (A) To pause the execution of a coroutine
(B) To resume the execution of a coroutine
(C) To specify a value to be returned from a function
(D) To pass data to another coroutine
questions.explanation: The keyword 'await' is used to pause the execution of a coroutine, allowing other tasks to run while waiting for the awaited task to complete.

questions.q: What is the difference between a function and an async function?
opts: (A) A function is a block of code, while an async function is a block of code that returns a promise
(B) A function is a reusable block of code, while an async function is a non-reusable block of code
(C) A function is a code that takes arguments, while an async function is a code that does not take arguments
(D) A function is a code that modifies variables, while an async function is a code that does not modify variables
questions.explanation: A function is a block of code that returns a value, while an async function is a block of code that returns a promise, which can be used to handle asynchronous operations.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the keyword 'async' in an async function?
opts: (A) To specify that a function is asynchronous
(B) To specify that a function is synchronous
(C) To specify that a function takes arguments
(D) To specify that a function does not take arguments
questions.explanation: The keyword 'async' is used to specify that a function is asynchronous, allowing it to return a promise and handle asynchronous operations.

questions.q: What is the difference between a function and a callback function?
opts: (A) A function is a block of code, while a callback function is a small block of code
(B) A function is a reusable block of code, while a callback function is a non-reusable block of code
(C) A function is a code that takes arguments, while a callback function is a code that does not take arguments
(D) A function is a code that modifies variables, while a callback function is a code that does not modify variables
questions.explanation: A function is a block of code that can be reused, while a callback function is a small block of code that is used to perform a simple operation, often as a parameter to another function.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the keyword 'call' in a function?
opts: (A) To invoke a function
(B) To pass data to a function
(C) To specify a value to be returned from a function
(D) To pass a function as an argument to another function
questions.explanation: The keyword 'call' is used to pass a function as an argument to another function, allowing the function to be invoked at a later time.
